description: same as SAM QNAME = sequencing event identifier; suffixed with /1 or /2 for unmerged paired reads
same as SAM FLAG = bit-encoded alignment metatdata
same as SAM RNAME = reference name i.e. chromosome
same as SAM POS1 = leftmost alignment position in RNAME
same as SAM MAPQ = mapping quality assigned by minimap2
same as SAM CIGAR = alignment CIGAR string
closest 1-indexed site on RNAME for the 5'-most base of this alignment in read order; signed by the direction from this base to SITE_POS1_1
1-indexed RE site coordinate on RNAME for SITE_INDEX1_1
signed distance in bp from the strand-adjusted (but NOT clip-adjusted) 5'-most base of the alignment to SITE_POS1_1
same as above for the 3'-most base of the alignment
same as above for the 3'-most base of the alignment
same as above for the 3'-most base of the alignment
unsigned SITE_INDEX1_2 for the actual or projected read 3' end
1-indexed RE site coordinate on RNAME for SEQ_SITE_INDEX1_2
boolean 0|1 flag; true if both ends of this read were validated to reach an adapter/RE site; 0==false for incomplete single reads or reads from unmerged pairs
boolean 0|1 flag; true if both ends of this insert were validated to reach an adapter/RE site; 0==false for incomplete single reads but 1==true for reads from unmerged pairs
encoded 5'-most  base on the insert as (+|- strand)(chromIndex1)(9-digit padded pos1 or sitePos1); used for deduplication
encoded 3'-most  (projected) base on the insert as above; not necessarily the true end of an insert if not projected and not and end-to-end insert
ONT channel tag = integer number of the pore that sequenced the read (0 if NA or unknown)
HiFiRe3 trim tags = trim5<comma>trim3
length of S_SEQ in bp for single or merged reads; internode distance for unmerged paired reads if no SV in read otherwise 0 as insert size is unknown
boolean 0|1 flag; true if INSERT_SIZE is between --min-allowed-size and twice that value
fastp merge status for paired reads as mergeLevel:nRead1:nRead2; 0:0:0 for single-read platforms
minimap2 de:f: tag = fractional gap-compressed per-base sequence divergence
custom hv:i: tag = bit-encoded flag of alignment and read variant status
number of reference bases spanned by the alignment (excludes CIGAR I operations)
number of read bases comprising the alignment (excludes CIGAR D operations)
number of bp from the clipped read 5' end to the 3' end of this alignment (read 5' ends are always validated ends of the insert)
number of bp from a validated read 3' end to the 5' end of this alignment; 0 if the read 3' end is not a validated end of the insert
boolean 0|1 flag; true if STEM5_LENGTH < --min-allowed-size
boolean 0|1 flag; true if STEM3_LENGTH <--min-allowed-size; false if STEM3_LENGTH == 0
reference vs. read traversal block; numbered sequentially across each read; alignments with the same BLOCK_N failed traversal check
bitwise encoding of the first failure flag applied to this alignment; 0 if the alignment passed all alignment-level quality filters
bitwise encoding of first failure flag applied to the junction following this alignment; 0 if the junction passed all end-to-end chimera and block traversal filters
bitwise encoding of whether the alignment and read overlapped a target region
boolean 0|1 flag; true if the 5' most (ONT adaptive) or any (other platforms) alignment of the read was on target; always 1 if not a targeted library
boolean 0|1 flag; true if the read has at least one SV junction where JXN_FAILURE_FLAG == 0; false if no junctions or all failed
same as SAM SEQ = read base sequence; S_SEQ S_QUAL and CS_TAG tag are large trailing fields that can be truncated away
same as SAM_QUAL = read base quailities; like S_SEQ is alignment-level due to possible reverse complement per alignment
minimap2 cs tag with read-to-genome alignment details (excluding clips)
